objects.name: Analytic 1644
objects.description: Correlates (1) an application obtaining or maintaining elevated control mechanisms capable of resisting removal (device administrator, accessibility control, managed-owner posture), (2) user navigation into uninstall or application-management flows, and (3) immediate UI redirection, back-navigation injection, modal dismissal, or failed uninstall completion followed by continued app presence. Defender observes a causal chain where a removal attempt is actively disrupted and the target application remains installed.

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: android:MDMLog
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: application enabled as device administrator, device owner, profile owner, or equivalent elevated management role before uninstall attempt

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: android:MDMLog
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: application granted accessibility service privileges capable of screen observation or global action invocation before removal attempt

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: application invokes accessibility global actions (back/home/recents) or observes package-management UI immediately after uninstall/settings screen becomes foreground

x_mitre_mutable_elements.description: Correlation window between uninstall UI entry, interference event, and continued install state
Set of elevated roles considered removal-resistant (device admin, owner modes, accessibility)
UI actions considered suspicious during uninstall flows (BACK, HOME, RECENTS)
Known legitimate accessibility services expected to use global actions
Number of repeated uninstall attempts before escalation
Outbound traffic threshold confirming continued meaningful activity after failed removal
